headline: Super Micro Deepens Mirantis Ties To Standardize Open Source AI Clouds
summary: Super Micro Computer (NasdaqGS:SMCI) is expanding its ecosystem partnerships to automate AI cloud infrastructure with Mirantis. The collaboration focuses on integrating open source platforms, including k0rdent AI, with NVIDIA based GPU cloud environments. The move targets sovereign AI and hybrid GPU cloud use cases, with an emphasis on operational automation and reduced manual provisioning. For investors watching AI infrastructure, this partnership illustrates how NasdaqGS:SMCI is...

headline: Arm to Sell Its Own Chips, Eyeing Sales Goal of $15 Billion
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Arm Holdings Plc, which made its name licensing technology to semiconductor makers, will begin selling its own chips for the first time, adding a business that it expects to generate about $15 billion annually within five years. Meta Platforms Inc. will be the first major customer for the company’s chip, called an AGI CPU, Arm said Tuesday at an event in San Francisco. The product will have as many as 136 cores — a measure of processing power — and draw 300 watts of electricity, A

headline: RAMageddon Arrives: AI’s Endless Appetite Just Killed the PC Comeback
summary: The global PC market has clawed its way back from a brutal slump in 2022 and 2023, when shipments cratered amid post-pandemic oversupply, with 2024 delivering modest growth as businesses and consumers began refreshing aging fleets. By 2025, the rebound appeared to be accelerating sharply — shipments rose 9.3% year-over-year, fueled by commercial upgrades to ... headline: Citi Cuts Its Target for Super Micro Computer to $25
summary: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) has had a brutal stretch. Shares have fallen nearly 21% over the past week, close to 30% over the past month and are down nearly 42% over the past year. headline: Why Super Micro Computer (SMCI) Is Down 29.5% After Indictment Over Alleged China AI Server Exports And What's Next
summary: In March 2026, U.S. prosecutors indicted three people connected to Super Micro Computer, including co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, over alleged illegal exports of Nvidia-powered AI servers to China, prompting his resignation from the board and the appointment of DeAnna Luna as acting Chief Compliance Officer. At the same time, Super Micro has been rolling out an expanded portfolio of Nvidia Blackwell- and Vera Rubin-based AI systems and deepening ecosystem partnerships, highlighting a...
